info.title: Multiview Control API
info.description: REST + WebSocket + SSE management API for the Multiview engine.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/account/audit` — the append-only account audit log, paginated
(role: read).
get.description:
Returns `{entries, next_cursor}`: entries oldest-first within the page,
resumable via `?cursor=next_cursor`, optionally `?filter=<kind>`. There is no
mutating verb on this resource — the log is append-only by construction.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Resume strictly after this `seq` cursor (from a previous `next_cursor`).
Absent fetches from the beginning of the log.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Restrict the listing to a single action kind (`kebab-case` slug).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The maximum number of entries to return (clamped to
[`MAX_AUDIT_PAGE_LIMIT`]; defaults to [`DEFAULT_AUDIT_PAGE_LIMIT`]).

200.description: A page of account-audit entries (oldest-first) + the resume cursor.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

get.description:
Returns the actions awaiting local execution or cancellation, oldest-first.
An action that has executed or been cancelled drops out of this list.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/actions/{id}/cancel` — cancel a pending action locally (role:
write).
post.description:
**Local always wins:** a pending action the operator cancels here is
cancelled, and any later (portal-fed) execution attempt is refused. An action
that has **already executed** answers `410 Gone` `already_executed` — the
truthful "too late", never a fake success. An unknown id is `404`. The cancel
is recorded in the append-only account audit store.

200.description: The action was pending and is now cancelled (local wins).

403.description: Not authorized to cancel.

410.description: The action has already executed — too late to cancel.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Keep only alarms at or above this X.733 severity (e.g. `major`).

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Keep only active (`true`) or only cleared/historical (`false`) alarms.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Keep only alarms whose scope kind matches (e.g. `tile`, `probe`).

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/alarms/{id}/ack` — acknowledge an alarm (role: write; If-Match).

403.description: Not authorized to acknowledge.

get.description:
**404-free**: an unconfigured deployment answers `200` with
`configured: false`, `routing: null`, and `selectable_tracks: ["prog"]`
(the program bus is always selectable). The response `ETag` is the document
version a later `PUT` must present as `If-Match`.

200.description: The audio-routing document (404-free: `configured: false` + a null document when none is set; ETag in the response header).

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/v1/audio-routing` — replace the singleton document (role: write;
`If-Match` required → `428`/`412`).

200.description: The replaced document (new ETag in the response header; X-Multiview-Apply: restart — it takes effect via config export + restart).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

422.description: The body is not a valid audio-routing document (detail names the field path or the violated routing invariant; references to undeclared sources are checked at config export, not here).

get.description: An optional `?object_id=` filters to a single object's history.

parameters.name: object_id
parameters.description: Restrict the listing to a single object id.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

post.description:
The stored layout body is resolved from the layouts repository and solved
**here, at request time** (off the engine hot path): an unknown id, a body
that does not parse as a `{canvas, layout, cells}` document, one that does
not solve (bad grid / geometry), a pinned-canvas mismatch (a Class-2
change — ADR-R004), or an unknown running canvas (no seeded snapshot — the
gate fails closed) is an honest `422` **before** any `202`. On `202` the
command carries the solved layout, so the engine's frame-boundary drain
only swaps (O(cells), no I/O — invariants #1/#10); the `202` body's
`applied_live`/`carried_only` arrays state which per-cell property classes
genuinely apply on screen. Idempotency reserves **before** resolution: a
replayed `Idempotency-Key` returns the original operation id (kind
`replay`, undecorated) without re-resolving, and a refused resolve
releases the key. The submit mirrors [`cmd_swap`] (shed-on-full) and never
blocks the engine (invariant #10); the outcome rides the realtime stream
as a `job.progress` event (ADR-W008).

202.description: Apply-layout accepted: the stored layout was resolved + solved and will swap in at the next frame boundary; `applied_live`/`carried_only` state the property classes. Outcome on the realtime stream (`job.progress`).

403.description: Not authorized to apply a layout.

422.description: The layout id does not exist, its stored body does not parse/solve, its canvas differs from the running session's pinned canvas (Class-2), or the running canvas is unknown (no seeded snapshot — the gate fails closed).

503.description: Engine command bus at capacity; shed.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/config/export` — render the live resource stores as a complete
`multiview.toml` document (ADR-W015).
get.description:
Composes the working layout (the id-sorted first layout whose body carries a
`canvas`) with every stored source/output/overlay/probe into a
[`multiview_config::MultiviewConfig`], validates the whole document, and
returns it as TOML. This closes the management loop honestly today: edit in
the UI → export → persist as the config file → the next start applies it.

403.description: Not authorized to read.

422.description: The stores do not compose into a valid configuration (detail names the violation).

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/devices/{id}` — fetch one device (role: read; per-object authz).

200.description: The device (ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to read this device.

200.description: The replaced device (new ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

422.description: The body is not a valid device document (detail names the field path).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/devices/{id}` — adopt/create a device (role: write).
post.description:
Validates the body against `multiview_config::Device` (`422` on an invalid
document), seeds the runtime status registry in `ADOPTING` so
`GET /devices/{id}/status` answers immediately, and **starts the device's
supervised driver poller** (DEV-A4) — which performs the first probe and
drives the device to `ONLINE`/`AUTH_FAILED`/`UNREACHABLE`. The poller is a
no-op for devices the factory does not manage (the default build / a
non-`zowietek` driver).

201.description: The adopted device (ETag in the response header; X-Multiview-Apply declares how it takes effect).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

422.description: The body is not a valid device document (detail names the field path).

delete.description:
Refused `409 /problems/conflict` while any Source or Output still references
this device via `device_ref` (ADR-M009): the problem detail names the
blocking resource so the operator knows what to unbind. The delete is never
partially applied — a refused delete leaves the device intact.

403.description: Not authorized to administer.

409.description: A Source/Output still references this device via device_ref.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/devices/{id}/identify` — flash the device's identify indicator
(role: write; fire-and-forget `204`).

403.description: Not authorized to identify.

get.description:
Returns the device's running driver's enumerated targets (DEV-A4: a
decoder-mode `zowietek` box's decode-table slots). Honestly empty until a
driver has enumerated — and on a build/device with no live driver — never
fabricated live telemetry.

200.description: The declared output targets (empty until a driver enumerates).

403.description: Not authorized to read this device.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/devices/{id}/probe` — re-probe the device now (role: write).
post.description:
A synchronous management verb (ADR-W017): it confirms the device exists and
acknowledges the probe request (`200`). The device's supervised driver poller
(DEV-A4) is already probing on its own ≤1 Hz cadence and re-probing on
reconnect, so the latest status is read via `GET /devices/{id}/status`; this
verb is the operator's explicit "I looked" acknowledgement.

403.description: Not authorized to probe.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/devices/{id}/reboot` — reboot the device (role: write; `202`).
post.description:
A long-running management verb (ADR-W017): the operation id is minted and
`202`'d; the outcome arrives on the realtime stream once the driver lands.

202.description: Reboot accepted; outcome on the realtime stream.

403.description: Not authorized to reboot.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/devices/{id}/set-mode` — converge the device to a mode
(role: write; `202` + operation id + declared DEV-class impact).
post.description:
The device-side impact is **declared in the body before apply** (ADR-M009):
the device restarts its pipeline; bound sources ride the tile ladder to
`NO_SIGNAL` during the switch; no Multiview output is interrupted. The route
mints the operation id, `202`s, and **dispatches** the convergence to the
device's running driver poller (DEV-A4), which records the requested mode as
its desired mode, runs `plan_mode_convergence` → `converge_mode`
(close-before-open), and publishes the `device.mode` outcome on the realtime
stream; a failed apply is re-converged on the poller's next adopt/reconnect
pass. When no live poller is running (the default build / a device with no
spawned driver), the `202` still declares the impact but nothing applies the
change: the device's configured `desired_mode` is what a later-spawned
poller converges onto when its adopt/reconnect reaches `ONLINE`.

202.description: Mode change accepted (impact declared); outcome on the realtime stream.

403.description: Not authorized to change the mode.

get.description:
Returns the device's running driver's enumerated candidates (DEV-A4: a
`zowietek` device's served RTSP mounts). Honestly empty until a driver has
enumerated — and on a build/device with no live driver — never fabricated
live telemetry.

200.description: The declared source candidates (empty until a driver enumerates).

403.description: Not authorized to read this device.

get.description:
Reads the latest-wins [`DeviceStatusRegistry`](crate::devices::DeviceStatusRegistry)
(never persisted/exported). A freshly-adopted device with no driver probe yet
sits in `ADOPTING`. `404` when the device is not adopted.

200.description: The device's latest-wins runtime status (never persisted).

403.description: Not authorized to read this device.

404.description: No device with that id is adopted.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/devices/{id}/test-pattern` — display a test pattern on the
device (role: write; fire-and-forget `204`).

403.description: Not authorized.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/discovery/devices` — the current untrusted inventory snapshot
(role: read).
get.description:
Returns the discovered services found by the most recent scans, AAAA-first
with IPv4 labelled legacy, stale (TTL-expired) rows purged on read. These are
**hints**, not devices: adopting one is the separate `POST /devices/{id}`
confirm-adopt referencing a discovered address (ADR-0041).

200.description: The untrusted discovery inventory (hints requiring explicit confirm-adopt; never devices).

403.description: Not authorized to read discovery.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/discovery/devices/scan` — kick a time-bounded mDNS browse
(role: write; `202` + operation id).
post.description:
The browse runs on a bounded control-plane task: it asks the injected browser
for services within a time budget, classifies each into the **untrusted
inventory** (AAAA-first, TTL-stamped), and publishes a `device.discovered`
event per service, correlated to this operation id via the envelope `corr`
(ADR-RT007). It never creates a device (ADR-0041).

Scans are **single-flight** (one in-flight browse — concurrent `mdns-sd`
browses corrupt each other's listeners/queriers, and one browse at a time is
the ADR-M008 rate limit): a request that arrives while a scan runs
**attaches** to it and is answered with the *running* scan's operation id.
A retried `Idempotency-Key` returns the original operation id without
re-executing the browse (the canonical replay semantics).

202.description: Scan accepted (or attached to the single-flight running scan, or replayed by Idempotency-Key — the operation id names the scan that actually runs); discovered rows stream as device.discovered events correlated via corr and land in the untrusted inventory.

403.description: Not authorized to scan.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/health` — list the active health warnings (role: read).

parameters.name: active
parameters.description:
Keep only active (`true`, the default) or include cleared (`false`)
warnings.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/inputs/{id}/streams` — the input's elementary-stream inventory
(role: read; per-object authz).
get.description:
Returns the cached [`StreamInventory`] for input `id` from the off-engine
snapshot (inv #10 — the output-clock thread is never touched). `404` when the
input is unknown or has not been probed yet.

403.description: Not authorized to read this input.

404.description: No such input, or it has not been probed yet.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/layouts/{id}` — fetch one layout (role: read; per-object authz).

200.description: The layout (ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to read this layout.

200.description: The replaced layout (new ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/layouts/{id}` — create a layout (role: write; per-object authz).

201.description: The created layout (ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to write.
Not authorized to administer.

get.description:
Always `200` (enforcement is **data**, never an error path): a `licensed:true`
resource with the computed ladder `state`/`enforcement` when a lease is
installed, a `licensed:false` resource otherwise.

200.description: The computed licence resource (enforcement is data; always 200).

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/licence/challenge` — the `<host>.challenge` export as an
`application/cbor` attachment (role: read).
get.description:
The challenge carries **only** salted digests + monotonic counters — never a
raw serial, MAC, or hostname (data minimisation, brief §3/§8). The digests +
counters are sourced from the entitlement plane; CONSPECT-1 renders the
machine's current installed-lease count and an empty salted-digest set when
the cli has not yet supplied a salted machine fingerprint (the cli wiring is
CONSPECT-10). The body is canonical CBOR a portal consumes byte-for-byte.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/licence/lease` — verify + install a presented signed lease
binding (CBOR body; role: write).
post.description:
Returns `200 {lease, valid_to}` on success, or an RFC 9457 problem on
rejection: `signature_invalid` (422), `fingerprint_mismatch` (409),
`lease_stale` (409). A binding that is not well-formed CBOR is a `422`
`malformed_binding` (bad-inputs-are-the-purpose). When no issuer key has been
pinned, the install is refused with a `409` `no_pinned_key`. A rejection never
degrades the machine — the previously-installed (or empty) state stays put
(fail toward leniency).

requestBody.description: The signed lease binding, canonical CBOR.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to install.

409.description: Fingerprint mismatch, stale grant, or no pinned key.

413.description: The binding body exceeds the size cap.

422.description: Malformed CBOR or an invalid signature.

get.description:
Always `200` (it is a data report, never an error path): the honest local
heartbeat status (transport + last/next contact + payload fields). With no
lease installed it reports `transport: "none"` and null contact instants. The
spec mandates **no** mutating endpoint exists for the heartbeat — this is
read-only, and the router wires only `GET`.

200.description: The honest local heartbeat status (read-only; no mutating endpoint exists).

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

get.description:
Each peer is a salted-digest id, an optional name (only once adopted), a
`claimed` flag, `last_seen` (seconds), and `relaying_for_us`. A peer is
**never** auto-trusted; relaying is an explicit operator confirm-adopt
(ADR-0041 doctrine). The ids are pure hex — never a raw identifier (brief §8).

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/v1/mesh/relay` — opt this machine in/out of relaying neighbours
(role: write).
put.description:
A real, persisted toggle: the new state is written to the shared mesh state
and the updated status is returned. Discovery is unaffected (always-on) — this
toggles only the **relay** opt-in (brief §9.2). Every successful toggle is
recorded in **both** trails: the change-audit log and — as an
actor-attributed [`AccountAuditKind::RelayToggle`](crate::account_audit::AccountAuditKind::RelayToggle)
entry — the append-only account-audit store (the spec's relay opt-in/out row,
brief §10/§11).

200.description: Relay opt-in updated; the new mesh status.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to toggle relay.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/mesh/status` — the always-on discovery + relay summary (role:
read).
get.description:
Always `200`: discovery is always-on, so this is a pure data report —
`{discovery, relay_enabled, role, via?, peers_count}`.

200.description: The always-on mesh discovery + relay status.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.
Authenticated but not authorized to read.

put.description:

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/salvos/{id}/cancel` — cancel an armed salvo (role: write; 202).

parameters.name: head

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/salvos/{id}/fire` — fire a named salvo through the command bus
(role: write) → `202 {action_id, queued_at}`.
post.description:
The named salvo must exist (else `404 salvo_unknown`). On success a `Salvo`
pending action is queued, the engine `TakeSalvo` command is submitted to the
bounded bus (a full bus sheds to `503`, never blocking the engine), the action
is marked executed, and the fire is recorded in the account audit store. The
action id is the pending-action id (the operator can correlate it on the
strip + the audit trail).

202.description: Fire queued + submitted; outcome on the realtime stream.

403.description: Not authorized to fire.

503.description: Engine command bus at capacity; shed.

parameters.name: head
parameters.description: The output head this recall targets, if scoped to one head.

403.description: Not authorized to take.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

200.description: The source (ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to read this source.

200.description: The replaced source (new ETag in the response header). X-Multiview-Apply declares how it takes effect: `live` for synthetic kinds applied to the running engine, `restart` otherwise (ADR-W018).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

422.description: The body is not a valid source document (detail names the field path).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/sources/{id}` — create a source (role: write; per-object authz).

201.description: The created source (ETag in the response header). X-Multiview-Apply declares how it takes effect: `live` for synthetic kinds (bars/solid/clock) applied to the running engine at a frame boundary, `restart` otherwise (ADR-W018).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

422.description: The body is not a valid source document (detail names the field path).

204.description: The source was deleted. X-Multiview-Apply: `live` when the running engine unregisters it at a frame boundary (bound tiles ride their failover slate), `restart` when no engine is draining (ADR-W018).

403.description: Not authorized to administer.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/support/bundle` — compose a previewable, redacted, media-free
context-pack (role: write; entitled tiers only). Composing performs **no
consent check** (§7.2): consent governs the daily outbound pipe, not a
deliberate operator attachment. Every compose lands a `bundle-compose`
account-audit entry. → `202 {bundle_id}`; read the preview at
`GET /support/bundle/{bundle_id}`.

202.description: The bundle was composed; read the preview by id.

403.description: Not authorized, or the free tier is not entitled.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/support/bundle/{id}` — read a composed bundle's preview (role:
read; entitled tiers only). Unknown/evicted id → `404`. The preview lists
every redaction and carries no media.

403.description: Not authorized, or the free tier is not entitled.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/support/data-request/{id}/approve` — approve an inbound egress
request **locally** (role: write). **Nothing leaves without this yes:** the
request transitions to `approved` and egress is gated on that state. An
already-expired request is `410 request_expired` (and stays expired). Unknown
id → `404`. Lands a `data-request-approve` account-audit entry.

403.description: Not authorized to approve.

410.description: The request window elapsed — too late to approve.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/support/data-request/{id}/deny` — deny an inbound egress request
locally (role: write). Nothing leaves. Symmetric to approve.

403.description: Not authorized to deny.

410.description: The request window elapsed — too late to deny.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/support/entitlement` — the tier-derived support routing (role:
read). Always `200`: the free tier gets `eligible:false` + the community
route + the one quiet line, an eligible tier gets its support queue + SLA.

403.description: Authenticated but not authorized to read.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/support/tickets` — list local ticket summaries newest-first
(role: read; entitled tiers only).

403.description: Not authorized, or the free tier is not entitled.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/support/tickets` — raise a local ticket (role: write; entitled
tiers only → else `403 not_entitled`). The store mints the `CS-xxxx` id, seeds
the thread with the opening body, and auto-attaches the machine context
(§7.1). Every raise lands a `ticket` account-audit entry. → `201 Created`.

201.description: The ticket was raised; the full thread is returned.

403.description: Not authorized, or the free tier is not entitled.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/support/tickets/{id}` — fetch one ticket (full thread) (role:
read; entitled tiers only). Unknown id → `404`.

200.description: The ticket with its full thread + machine context.

403.description: Not authorized, or the free tier is not entitled.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/support/tickets/{id}/close` — close a ticket locally (role:
write; entitled tiers only). Idempotent (re-closing an already-closed ticket
is still `200`). Unknown id → `404`. → `200`.

403.description: Not authorized, or the free tier is not entitled.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/support/tickets/{id}/reply` — append a reply to a ticket's
thread (role: write; entitled tiers only). A reply to a **closed** ticket is
`409 ticket_closed` (pinned). Unknown id → `404`. Each reply lands a `ticket`
account-audit entry. → `200` with the updated thread.

200.description: The reply appended; the updated thread is returned.

403.description: Not authorized, or the free tier is not entitled.

409.description: The ticket is closed — replies are refused.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/sync-groups` — list all sync groups, id-sorted (role: read).

403.description: Not authorized to read.

200.description: The sync group (ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to read this group.

404.description: No sync group with that id.

200.description: The replaced sync group (new ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

404.description: No sync group with that id.

422.description: The body is not a valid sync-group document (detail names the field path).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/sync-groups/{id}` — create a sync group (role: write).
post.description:
Validates the body against `multiview_config::SyncGroup` (`422` on an invalid
document, e.g. an empty member list).

201.description: The created sync group (ETag in the response header).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

422.description: The body is not a valid sync-group document (detail names the field path).

403.description: Not authorized to administer.

404.description: No sync group with that id.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/sync-groups/{id}/measure` — kick off a skew measurement
(role: write; `202` + operation id).
post.description:
In this slice (no driver actor) the operation id is minted and `202`'d; the
measurement result (`device.sync`) arrives on the realtime stream once the
driver actors land (DEV-A4/A5). A retried `Idempotency-Key` returns the
original id.

202.description: Measurement accepted; result on the realtime stream.

403.description: Not authorized to measure.

404.description: No sync group with that id.

403.description: Not authorized to read.

put.description:
Submits a `SetTallyOverride` command to the engine and records the request in
the control-plane override registry. The engine *applies* the override; this
endpoint reports `202` (the resolved state arrives later on the realtime
stream as a `tally.state` event).

202.description: Override accepted; outcome on the realtime stream.

403.description: Not authorized to write.

503.description: Engine command bus at capacity; shed.

202.description: Clear accepted; outcome on the realtime stream.

403.description: Not authorized to write.

503.description: Engine command bus at capacity; shed.

403.description: Not authorized to read.

put.description:
On create returns `201`; on replace enforces `If-Match` (→ `412`) and returns
`200`. The body is validated by [`TallyProfile::validate`] first; the path
`id` is authoritative.

403.description: Not authorized to write.

patch.summary:
`PATCH /x-nmos/connection/v1.1/single/receivers/{id}/staged` — stage (and,
for an immediate activation, apply) a connection on a receiver (role: write).

403.description: Not authorized to write.

AcceptedBody.description: A `202 Accepted` body returned for an asynchronously-applied command.

applied_live.description:
For `apply-layout` (ADR-W019): the per-cell property classes the live
apply genuinely applies at the next frame boundary (e.g. `geometry`,
`bindings`, `z_order`, `opacity`, `on_loss`). Absent on other commands.

carried_only.description:
For `apply-layout` (ADR-W019): the property classes that are **carried**
in the stored document (persisted, exported) but not yet rendered by the
compositor (e.g. `border`, `qos`, `fit`). Absent on other commands.

operation_id.description:
The operation id correlating this command's eventual outcome on the
realtime stream.

AccountAuditEntry.description:
One immutable account-audit entry: who did what, and when.

The four spec fields (brief §11: `{at, actor, kind, detail}`) plus a
monotonic `seq` cursor the store assigns on append so pagination is stable
and resumable even when two entries share an `at` timestamp.

actor.description:
The authenticated principal that performed the action (its key id), or a
well-known system actor for machine-originated events — the **actor**.

at_nanos.description:
The media-timeline timestamp (nanoseconds) the action was recorded — the
**when** (`at`).

detail.description:
An optional structured detail (e.g. the lease serial, the salvo name, the
action id). Never contains secrets or raw identifiers (brief §8).

kind.description: The kind of account action — the **kind**.

seq.description:
The monotonic sequence number the store assigned on append — the stable
pagination cursor. Strictly increasing; never reused.

AccountAuditKind.description:
The kind of account-side action an [`AccountAuditEntry`] records.

Each variant names a distinct auditable account action from ADR-0053 §4 +
the brief §10. Serialised `kebab-case` so the slug is stable across the
machine and the portal. `#[non_exhaustive]` so a future account action adds a
variant without breaking the wire contract.

AccountAuditPage.description: A page of account-audit entries plus the cursor to resume from.

entries.description: The entries in this page, oldest-first within the page (ascending `seq`).

next_cursor.description:
The cursor to pass as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page, or `None` when
this page reached the end of the log. Opaque to clients (the `seq` of the
last returned entry).

oneOf.description: Not yet acknowledged by an operator.
Acknowledged: who acknowledged it and when (media-time nanoseconds).

when.description: When the acknowledgement happened, media-time nanoseconds.

who.description: Operator/user identifier that acknowledged the alarm.

AckStateDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_core::alarm::AckState`].

Serde-equivalent: internally tagged on `state`, `PascalCase` variant tags; the
acknowledged variant carries `who` and `when` (nanoseconds).

Activation.description: An IS-05 **activation** block: when a staged change takes effect.

oneOf.description: The activation mode, or [`None`] to stage without activating.

requested_time.description:
The requested activation time (TAI `<seconds>:<nanoseconds>`), for a
scheduled mode.

ActivationMode.description: The activation mode of an IS-05 connection change.

AlarmKindDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_core::alarm::AlarmKind`] (probe/fault taxonomy).

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered as its `PascalCase` variant name.

dwell.description: How long the condition has persisted, media-time nanoseconds.

id.description: Stable identity of this alarm instance.

kind.description: The probe/fault class that raised it.

raised_at.description: When the alarm was first raised, media-time nanoseconds.

id.description: Probe identifier.

oneOf.description: A multiview tile, by zero-based index.

AlarmScopeDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_core::alarm::AlarmScope`].

Serde-equivalent: internally tagged on `kind`, `snake_case` variant tags.

layout.description: The layout id to make active on the running multiview.

AudioRouteDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::audio::AudioRoute` — one per-input
route in the audio-routing document.

channels.description: The channel layout requested for this input.

gain_db.description: Program-bus contribution gain in dB (`0.0` ⇒ unity; must be finite).

include_in_program_bus.description: Whether this input contributes to the mixed program bus.

input_id.description: The managed source id (`sources[].id`) this route takes audio from.

language.description: ISO-639 language tag advertised for the discrete track (e.g. `"eng"`).

mute.description:
Whether this input is muted on the program bus (its discrete track, if
any, stays declared).

target_track.description:
The named discrete output track (absent ⇒ program bus only). `"prog"`
is reserved for the mixed program bus.

AudioRoutingDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::AudioRouting` — the body accepted by
`PUT /api/v1/audio-routing` (the whole-document `[audio]` block).

sample_rate_hz.description: The working/program-bus sample rate in Hz (exact integer, > 0).

AudioRoutingStateDoc.description:
The response envelope of `GET`/`PUT /api/v1/audio-routing`.

The GET is **404-free**: an unconfigured deployment answers
`configured: false` with a `null` document and `selectable_tracks` of just
the always-available program bus `"prog"`.

configured.description: Whether an audio-routing document is configured.

oneOf.description: The routing document, or `null` when unconfigured.

selectable_tracks.description:
`"prog"` + every declared discrete track, in declaration order — the
set per-output `audio.tracks` selections resolve against.

AuditAction.description: The kind of mutation an audit entry records.

AuditEntry.description: One immutable audit-log entry: who did what to which object, and when.

action.description: The kind of mutation — part of the **what**.

actor.description:
The authenticated principal that performed the mutation (its key id /
JWT subject) — the **who**.

at_nanos.description:
The media-timeline timestamp (nanoseconds) the mutation was recorded —
the **when**.

detail.description:
An optional structured detail (e.g. the new resource body, or command
parameters). Never contains secrets.

object_id.description: The addressed object id — the rest of the **what**.

object_kind.description: The resource collection the object belongs to (e.g. `layout`).

bit.description: The zero-based bit position in the incoming tally word.

color.description: The lamp colour asserted when the bit is set.

Bundle.description:
One composed support bundle: the echoed request, the redacted/diagnostic
sections, and the list of every redaction the config pass made. **Carries no
media** by construction — there is no field that could hold a frame/picture.

composed_at_nanos.description: The media-timeline instant (nanoseconds) the bundle was composed.

config.description:
The redacted config sections, present iff `config` was requested. A JSON
object keyed by resource collection (`sources`/`outputs`/…); every secret
has been removed and every URL masked.

oneOf.description:
The utilisation/metrics summary, present iff `metrics` or `diagnostics`
was requested.

incidents.description:
The incident markers in the window, present iff `incidents` (or
`diagnostics`) was requested.

redactions.description:
Every redaction the config pass made (the masking the operator sees).
Empty when nothing was masked; never omitted (the surface is uniform).

window.description: The reporting window token the bundle was composed over.

BundleAccepted.description: The `202` body of a successful compose: the id to read the preview back under.

bundle_id.description: The composed bundle's id (read the preview at `GET .../bundle/{bundle_id}`).

BundleInclude.description:
Which diagnostic section a bundle includes. Serialised `lowercase` so the slug
is stable across the machine and the (later) portal sync. `#[non_exhaustive]`
so a future section is additive.

BundleRequest.description:
The `POST /api/v1/support/bundle` request body: the reporting `window` and the
sections to `include`.

include.description: The sections to include (deduplicated by the composer).

BundleWindow.description:
The time window a bundle reports over (spec §7.2). Serialised as the operator-
facing token (`1h` / `24h` / `7d`). `#[non_exhaustive]` so a future window is
additive.

oneOf.description: An auxiliary bus, by zero-based index.
An isolated (ISO) record bus, by zero-based index.

BusSourceDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_core::tally::BusSource`].

Serde-equivalent: internally tagged on `kind`, `snake_case` variant tags.

CancelledBody.description: The `200` body of a successful cancel.

cancelled.description: Always `true` on the `200` path — the action is cancelled.

oneOf.description: Auto-select the first usable caption track.
A subtitle track by stream id or language tag.

id.description: The track identifier.

ChannelMap.description:
An IS-08 **map**: each named output channel → its [`ChannelSource`].

A `BTreeMap` so the serialised map and any listing is deterministically
ordered (stable diagnostics + golden tests).

inputs.description: The set of known input ids this map may reference.

ChannelSource.description:
One output channel's source: an input id + the zero-based channel within it,
or `None`/silence when the channel is unrouted.

channel_index.description: The zero-based channel index within that input (ignored when silent).

input.description: The input the channel is fed from, or [`None`] for silence.

target.description: The tally target whose override is cleared.

primaries.description: Primaries axis (`auto` or an explicit primaries token).

document.description: The document to commit as the next revision.

ConfigRevision.description: One immutable revision of a target's config/layout document.

author.description: The principal that committed this revision (its key id / subject).

message.description: A short human commit message (e.g. `"expand to 9 cells"`).

target.description: The stable target key this revision belongs to (e.g. `layout:wall`).

ConnectionRequest.description: An IS-05 connection request `PATCH`ed to a `/staged` endpoint.

master_enable.description: Whether the sender/receiver is enabled once active.

sender_id.description: For a receiver: the sender id to subscribe to (the connection target).

transport_file.description: For a receiver: the SDP transport file describing the sender's stream.

transport_params.description: One transport-param block per leg (1 for single-path, 2 for ST 2022-7).

ConnectionState.description:
The IS-05 connection state of one endpoint: its current active params and any
pending staged change.

active.description: The currently-active transport params (empty until first activated).

oneOf.description: A staged-but-not-yet-active change, if one is pending.

staged_at.description:
The TAI instant (`<seconds>:<nanoseconds>`) the pending change was staged
at, captured so an `ActivateScheduledRelative` offset has a base to
resolve against. [`None`] when nothing is staged, or when the stage was
recorded without a clock stamp.

DataRequest.description:
One inbound data request awaiting **local approval**: support (or the licence
server, over the later transport) asks for additional data; nothing leaves
until the operator approves it here (ADR-0053 §3).

detail.description:
An optional structured detail (e.g. the ticket the request relates to).
Never the requested data.

request_id.description: The request id (the `DR-xxxx` / portal correlation key when synced).

requested_at_nanos.description: The media-timeline timestamp (nanoseconds) the request was recorded.

what.description:
A human-readable description of what is being requested (never the data
itself — this is the *ask*, not the payload).

DataRequestActioned.description:
The `200` body of a data-request approve/deny: the request's new terminal
state.

DataRequestState.description:
The lifecycle state of an inbound data request. Approve/deny are one-way; an
elapsed (un-actioned) request is `Expired` (`410 request_expired`).

DetectionZoneDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::DetectionZone` (normalized
sub-rectangle of a tile, `0.0..=1.0` on both axes).

node_id.description: The id of the node hosting this device.

receivers.description: The receiver ids this device exposes.

senders.description: The sender ids this device exposes.

Device.description: An IS-04 **Device**: a logical grouping of senders/receivers on a node.

DeviceAuthDoc.description: `OpenAPI` mirror of the `[devices.auth]` block: a write-only secret reference.

DeviceBodyDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_config::Device`] (ADR-M008): the config-as-code
managed-device document.

Serde-equivalent to the config type field-for-field; the runtime status is a
separate read-only projection ([`DeviceStatusDoc`]), never carried here.

address.description:
Management address, IPv6-first (e.g. `http://[fd00:db8::42]`). Required
for `zowietek`/`cast`; optional for `displaynode`.

alarm_on_offline.description:
The lowercase X.733 severity raised when the device stays offline
(`warning`/`minor`/`major`/`critical`); absent disables the alarm.

oneOf.description: Write-only credentials (the export emits the ref string only).

desired_mode.description: The desired converged work mode (driver vocabulary, e.g. `decoder`).

driver.description: The compiled-in driver family managing this device.

id.description: Stable device id; may be omitted (the path id is injected).

oneOf.description: Supervised-reconnect backoff bounds; absent uses the driver defaults.

DeviceDriverDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_config::DeviceDriver`] (ADR-M008): the
compiled-in device-driver families, `snake_case` on the wire.

stable_id.description: The vendor's stable device handle (UUID / PCI bus id / registryID).

DeviceStatusDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_events::DeviceStatus`] (ADR-M008 §2.1): the
read-only latest-wins runtime status `GET /devices/{id}/status` returns.

Serde-equivalent to the event type; runtime telemetry only — never persisted
or exported (config-as-code carries the durable desired state).

device_id.description: The registry device id this snapshot describes.

last_seen_ts.description: Engine monotonic nanoseconds the device last answered, once it has.

mode.description: The device's current converged mode (driver vocabulary), once known.

state.description:
The device's lifecycle state, uppercase on the wire (e.g. `ADOPTING`,
`ONLINE`, `DEGRADED`, `AUTH_FAILED`, `UNREACHABLE`).

temperature_c.description: Device-reported temperature (°C), where the vendor exposes it.

Diagnostics.description: The utilisation/diagnostics summary a bundle carries over its window.

cpu_p0.description: CPU busy-fraction floor (p0) over the window.

cpu_p100.description: CPU busy-fraction ceiling (p100) over the window.

cpu_p50.description: CPU busy-fraction median (p50) over the window.

cpu_p95.description: CPU busy-fraction 95th percentile over the window.

reconnects.description: The number of per-input reconnects recorded in the window.

samples.description: Total utilisation samples folded across the window.

shed_events.description: The number of shed-load events recorded in the window.

DiscoveredEndpoint.description:
One resolved management endpoint of a discovered service: a presentation-
ready address and its family. IPv6 is the lead family (ADR-0042); IPv4 is
labelled [`AddressFamily::Ipv4Legacy`]. IPv6 literals are bracketed so the
`host:port` form is URL-safe.

family.description:
The address family — `ipv6` (lead) or `ipv4-legacy`. The shared
[`AddressFamily`] enum carries no `ToSchema`, so the schema models the
wire string directly (the same `ipv6` / `ipv4-legacy` tokens serde emits).

DiscoveredService.description:
One **untrusted** discovery-inventory row: a classified, AAAA-first service
requiring explicit confirm-adopt (ADR-0041). It carries **no registry id** —
it is not a device.

endpoints.description: The management endpoints, **AAAA-first** (IPv6 lead, IPv4 legacy).

key.description:
The dedup key (driver kind + instance name + service type). Two sightings
of the same service share a key and the newer replaces the older.

last_seen_unix_ns.description: When this row was last seen (Unix nanoseconds), informational for the UI.

primary_address.description:
The primary management address — the first (IPv6, if any) endpoint's
`host:port`. This is the address an operator references when confirming
adoption via `POST /devices/{id}`.

txt.description: The decoded TXT records (advertised metadata), key-sorted.

DiscoveryDriverKind.description:
The inferred driver family of a discovered service, from its service type
(and the operator-configured zowietek-control type, if any).

A **closed** `#[non_exhaustive]` enum: a new discoverable family is a new
variant plus a wired browse type, never an open registry. `Unknown` is the
honest catch-all — a service we browsed but cannot classify is reported as
such, never guessed into a family.

DiscoveryModeDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_mesh::DiscoveryMode`] (Conspect, ADR-0051 §2).

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered `snake_case`. There is exactly **one**
value — `always_on` — and no way to set any other: discovery runs whenever the
account plane runs (the spec's *locked* row, no off switch exists).

DocumentDiff.description:
The added / removed / changed top-level keys between two documents.

A pragmatic, UI-facing structural diff over JSON objects: it reports which
top-level keys were added, removed, or whose value changed. Non-object
documents (or a type change at the root) are reported as a single synthetic
`<root>` change so the diff is always total.

added.description: Keys present in the new document but not the old (sorted).

changed.description: Keys present in both whose value differs (sorted).

removed.description: Keys present in the old document but not the new (sorted).

down_ms.description: Milliseconds the condition must clear before the alarm **clears**.

up_ms.description: Milliseconds the condition must persist before the alarm **raises**.

EnforcementLevelDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_licence::EnforcementLevel`].

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered `kebab-case`. Every level keeps a
running program **on air** (ADR-0050 §6.3, invariant #1) — enforcement is
data the surface renders, never a control-flow decision.

FiredBody.description: The `202` body of a successful salvo fire.

action_id.description: The queued action id (also the pending-action + audit correlation id).

queued_at_nanos.description:
The media-timeline timestamp (nanoseconds, from the ack-clock) the fire
was queued — the audit entry and the pending action share this instant.

FirstLine.description:
The first-line support route a tier maps to (spec §11): a real support queue
for eligible tiers, or the community channel for the free tier.

oneOf.description: No GPU cap — the `over_gpu` reason can never fire.
At most this many GPUs may be in use before the `over_gpu` reason fires.

value.description: At most this many GPUs may be in use before the `over_gpu` reason fires.

GpuLimitDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_licence::GpuLimit`].

Serde-equivalent: adjacently tagged on `kind`, value under `value`,
`snake_case` tags (`{"kind":"unlimited"}` / `{"kind":"limited","value":2}`).

HardwareClassDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_licence::HardwareClass`].

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered `snake_case`.

detected.description: The class detected on the machine (a mismatch is a ladder reason).

licensed.description: The class the entitlement is licensed for.

HealthWarningDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_events::HealthWarning`] (SA-0).

The body of `GET /api/v1/health`: an actionable health warning carrying a
stable `code` + a `remediation`. A round-trip test (`tests/health.rs`) pins
this shape to the real `multiview_events::HealthWarning` so they cannot drift.

active.description: Whether the condition is currently active.

code.description: The stable catalog code — the dedupe key the store + UI coalesce on.

message.description: A clear, human-readable description of the condition.

remediation.description: The concrete remediation — what the operator must do to fix it.

since.description: When the condition was first raised (engine monotonic nanoseconds).

HeartbeatStatus.description:
The read-only heartbeat-status surface (`GET /api/v1/licensing/heartbeat-status`).

With no licence-server client yet (blocked on the external wire-protocol doc,
brief §14 O1), this reports **honestly from local state**: the transport the
active lease arrived over, the lease install instant as `last_at`, the lease's
`next_contact_due` as `next_due`, and the exhaustive payload-field list. There
is **no** mutating endpoint for the heartbeat (the spec mandates none) — the
`POST /api/v1/account/licence/heartbeat` force-now action is a later,
feature-gated item (brief §11 #4), not this read surface.

last_at.description:
The instant this machine last accepted a lease (the install instant), RFC
3339; `None` when no lease is installed (no heartbeat yet — honest).

next_due.description:
When the next licensing contact is due (the active lease's
`next_contact_due`), RFC 3339; `None` when no lease is installed.

payload_fields.description:
The exhaustive list of fields a heartbeat payload carries
([`HEARTBEAT_PAYLOAD_FIELDS`]) — reported, never raw identifiers (§8).

transport.description:
The transport the active lease arrived over: `"direct"` (online server
contact), `"relay"` (mesh relay), or `"file"` (a dropped offline lease).
`"none"` when no lease is installed.

Incident.description: One incident marker in a bundle (a diagnostic event — never media).

at_unix_seconds.description: The Unix second the incident was recorded.

subject.description: What the incident applied to (input id, `program`, `system`, …).

cell.description: The cell id the index resolves to.

index.description: The zero-based source/display index in the tally protocol.

Is10Claims.description:
An IS-10 JWT **claims set** (the decoded token body).

Carries the registered claims IS-10 mandates plus the NMOS `x-nmos-api`
claim. Times are Unix seconds (the JWT `NumericDate`).

aud.description:
The audience (the resource server this token is for) — `aud`.

IS-10 allows a single audience or a list; a single value is modelled as a
one-element list on decode by the caller.

LadderStateDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_licence::LadderState`] (the seven conditions).

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered `snake_case`.

Layout.description:
A persisted layout resource.

The `body` is the opaque, validated layout document the editor produces; the
control plane stores and version-stamps it without interpreting its
internals here. (Engine-side validation against `multiview-config` happens
before a layout is applied.)

body.description: The opaque layout document (canvas + cells), as canonical JSON.

id.description: Stable resource id.

LayoutInput.description:
The fields a create/update accepts (the id is supplied separately on create
and immutable on update).

LeaseDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_licence::Lease`] (the dated entitlement
bounds, ADR-0050 §4).

Serde-equivalent: the dated fields are RFC 3339 date-time strings (how
`chrono`'s `DateTime<Utc>` serialises), the day counts are integers.

grace_days.description: The number of grace days that follow expiry.

grace_until.description: The end of the grace window (RFC 3339).

hard_at.description: The absolute hard bound from grant (RFC 3339).

next_contact_due.description: When the next heartbeat is due (RFC 3339).

serial.description: The opaque lease serial (server-issued; not a hardware identifier).

source.description: Where this grant came from (drives the term).

LeaseInstalled.description:
The `200` body of a successful `POST /api/v1/licence/lease` install: the
installed lease's serial and its `valid_to` (the lease term expiry, RFC 3339).

serial.description: The serial of the lease that was verified + installed.

valid_to.description: The instant the installed lease term expires (RFC 3339).

LeaseInstalledDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` body of a successful `POST /api/v1/licence/lease` install: the
installed lease's serial + its `valid_to` (the lease's `expires_at`, RFC
3339). Mirrors the [`crate::routes::licence::LeaseInstalled`] handler shape.

serial.description: The serial of the lease that was verified + installed.

valid_to.description: The instant the installed lease term expires (RFC 3339).

LeaseSourceDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_licence::LeaseSource`].

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered `snake_case` (`online`/`relay`/`file`).

LicenceResource.description:
The computed licence resource rendered at `GET /api/v1/licence`.

A single shape that always serialises (never `5xx`): `licensed` is `true` with
the full computed `status` when a verified lease is installed, and `false`
with `status: null` when none is — an honest "unlicensed" data report rather
than an error. The control plane, the chrome banner, and the portals all read
the same computed `status.enforcement` level; there is no second opinion.

licensed.description: Whether a verified lease is currently installed.

oneOf.description: The computed licence status when a lease is installed; `None` otherwise.

LicenceStatusDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_licence::LicenceStatusView`] — the computed
licence resource `GET /api/v1/licence` renders.

Serde-equivalent to the real view. The `state`/`enforcement` are **computed**
(the ladder, off the hot loop); every value keeps a running program on air.

blocks_new_instances.description: Whether the startup gate should refuse a new engine instance (S1).

config_locked.description: Whether the engine should deny hot-reconfiguration (derived, S2).

enforcement.description: The canonical enforcement level derived from the state.

gpu_limit.description: The GPU allowance carried by the entitlement.

gpus_in_use.description: The number of GPUs currently in use (informs the `over_gpu` reason only).

lease.description: The dated lease this status reflects.

reasons.description: Machine-readable reason codes the UI renders.

state.description: The computed ladder state (the seven conditions).

tier.description: The opaque commercial tier (rendered, never computed).

watermark.description: Whether the engine should stamp a corner watermark (derived, S3).

MediaFormat.description: The media format of a sender/receiver (the IS-04 `format` URN family).

MeshPeerDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_mesh::Peer`] — a single entry in the untrusted
discovered-peer inventory `GET /api/v1/mesh/peers` returns.

Serde-equivalent to the real peer. The `key` is the salted-digest **hex** id
(64 chars, never a raw identifier — brief §8); `name` is present only once the
operator has confirm-adopted + named the peer; `last_seen` is whole seconds;
`relaying_for_us` is an operator-set adoption flag, never auto (the peer is
**untrusted** until explicitly confirm-adopted — ADR-0041 doctrine).

claimed.description: Whether the peer advertised itself as claimed (observed from the announce).

key.description: The peer's salted-digest id (64-char lowercase hex; never a raw identifier).

last_seen.description: The whole-seconds monotonic instant the peer was last seen.

name.description: An operator-assigned name, present only once the peer is adopted + named.

relaying_for_us.description:
Whether THIS machine relays for the peer — set only by explicit operator
confirm-adopt, never by observation (untrusted inventory).

oneOf.description: Own internet path; not relaying for neighbours.
Online and opted-in to relay an offline neighbour's heartbeat.
Offline; leafing through an adopted relaying neighbour.

via.description:
The peer this machine relays its heartbeat through (salted-digest hex
id — never a raw identifier).

MeshRoleDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_mesh::MeshRole`] (Conspect, ADR-0051 §4).

Serde-equivalent: **internally tagged** on `kind` (`direct`/`relay`/`leaf`,
kebab) — never untagged (conventions §5). The `leaf` variant carries the `via`
peer (a salted-digest hex id) it leafs its heartbeat through.

MeshStatusDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_mesh::MeshStatus`] — the always-on mesh
discovery + relay summary `GET /api/v1/mesh/status` (and the `PUT
/api/v1/mesh/relay` reply) renders.

Serde-equivalent to the real status (pinned byte-for-byte by a round-trip test
in `tests/mesh.rs`). The mesh crate carries no `utoipa` dependency, so this
module owns the `OpenAPI` contract. Discovery is always-on (no off switch);
`via` is present only when the role is `leaf`.

discovery.description: Discovery is always-on (the only value; no off switch exists).

peers_count.description: How many peers are in the untrusted discovered-peer inventory.

relay_enabled.description: Whether this machine relays neighbours' heartbeats (the opt-in toggle).

via.description:
The peer this machine leafs through, present only when the role is `leaf`
(a salted-digest hex id).

NmosAccess.description: The NMOS access level granted for one API (the `x-nmos-api` value vocabulary).

NmosApiClaim.description: The NMOS-private `x-nmos-api` claim: per-API access grants.

href.description: The HTTP(S) hrefs the node is reachable at (its API base URLs).

Node.description: An IS-04 **Node**: the top-level host (a running Multiview instance).

OperationId.description:
A correlation id for an asynchronously-applied control command.

Returned to the client in a `202 Accepted` response and echoed by the engine
on the realtime stream (`corr`) when the command's outcome is known.

tracks.description: The selectable-track list (used only in `tracks` mode).

id.description: Stable operator id; may be omitted (the path id is injected).
Stable operator id; may be omitted.
Stable operator id; may be omitted.
Stable operator id; may be omitted.
Stable operator id; may be omitted.
Stable operator id; may be omitted.

oneOf.description: AES67 / ST 2110-30 audio-over-IP send (raw PCM multicast, no encode).

depth.description: PCM depth: `L24` (Class A interop default) or `L16`.

oneOf.description: Always absent for AES67 (raw PCM, no encode stage).

id.description: Stable operator id; may be omitted.

label.description: Display name (no mount/path/url to derive one from).

ptime_ms.description: Packet time in milliseconds (`1` = Class A).

OutputBodyDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::Output` (tagged by `kind`) — the body
accepted by `POST`/`PUT /api/v1/outputs/{id}`.

OutputTarget.description:
One decode-side entry point on a device, bindable as an ordinary managed
Output with a `device_ref` (ADR-M009 facet (b)).

id.description: A stable id for this target within the device (driver-assigned).

kind.description: The transport kind the device decodes (`rtsp` / `srt` / `rtmp` / `ndi`).

label.description: A human-friendly label for the decode slot, if the driver reports one.

OverlayBodyDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::Overlay` — the body accepted by
`POST`/`PUT /api/v1/overlays/{id}`.

Overlay kinds carry a large, kind-dependent parameter set captured verbatim
(lossless round-trip), so the schema documents the common envelope and
leaves the per-kind extras additive.

id.description: Stable overlay id; may be omitted (the path id is injected).

target.description: Attachment target (`canvas` or a cell id).
The tally target the override applies to.

action_id.description: The opaque, unique action id (a UUID) the cancel/execute paths address.

detail.description:
An optional structured detail (e.g. the salvo name a `salvo` action
fires). Never contains secrets.

requested_at_nanos.description: The media-timeline timestamp (nanoseconds) the action was queued.

requested_by.description: The principal (or portal/system actor) that requested the action.

PendingActionKind.description:
The kind of remote action queued for local approval/execution.

Serialised `kebab-case` so the slug is stable across the machine and the
portal. `#[non_exhaustive]` so a future action kind adds a variant without
breaking the wire contract.

PendingActionState.description:
The lifecycle state of a queued action.

An action is `Pending` until it is either `Executed` (it ran) or `Cancelled`
(the operator cancelled it locally first). Both terminal states are immutable
once reached — the local cancel cannot be undone by a later execution, and an
executed action cannot be retroactively cancelled (local-always-wins applies
only *before* execution).

PerceivedSeverityDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_core::alarm::PerceivedSeverity`] (X.733).

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered as its `PascalCase` variant name.

allOf.description: The kind-specific payload (`kind` + its fields sit at top level).

cell.description: The cell id this probe watches.

dwell.description: Dwell windows (raise/clear debounce; defaults to 1 s each way).

id.description: Stable probe id; may be omitted (the path id is injected).

latched.description: Whether the alarm latches (held until explicitly reset).

severity.description: The perceived severity (X.733) asserted when this probe fires.

ProbeBodyDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::Probe` — the body accepted by
`POST`/`PUT /api/v1/probes/{id}`.

luma_threshold.description: Luma ceiling (8-bit, `0..=255`) at or below which a pixel is "black".

zone.description: Detection zone within the tile (defaults to the full frame).

difference_threshold.description:
Inter-frame difference floor (per-mille, `0..=1000`) below which the
picture counts as frozen.

zone.description: Detection zone within the tile (defaults to the full frame).

oneOf.description: Silence detection on the cell's audio.

level_dbfs.description: Level ceiling in dBFS at or below which audio counts as silent.

oneOf.description: Loudness-violation detection against a compliance target.

ProbeKindDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::ProbeKind` (tagged by `kind`,
`snake_case`, flattened into the probe body).

Problem.description:
An RFC 9457 problem-details document.

Serialized as `application/problem+json`. The `status` field is authoritative
and is also used as the HTTP status code by the [`IntoResponse`] impl. A
`status` outside the valid `100..=599` range falls back to `500`.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

instance.description:
A URI reference identifying this specific occurrence (e.g. the request
path or an operation id).

status.description: The HTTP status code, duplicated into the body per RFC 9457.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

type.description: A URI reference identifying the problem type (here a `/problems/<slug>`).

attachments.description:
Attachment ids the operator references (recorded on the opening update's
context; the deliberate-attachment path is the bundle composer). Optional.

body.description: The opening body (seeds the append-only thread).

device_id.description: The id of the device this receiver belongs to.

format.description: The media format this receiver consumes.

subscribed_sender.description: The id of the sender this receiver is subscribed to, if connected.

Receiver.description: An IS-04 **Receiver**: an ingress flow (a Multiview input).

ReconnectPolicyDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of the `[devices.reconnect]` block: supervised-reconnect
backoff bounds.

Redaction.description:
What a single redaction removed/masked, surfaced in the preview so the
operator sees exactly what was taken out (never the value itself).

path.description:
The dotted path of the redacted field (e.g. `cam-1.auth.secret_ref`). A
location, never the value.

reason.description:
Why it was redacted: `secret` (a reference-only secret, removed) or `url`
(a transport URL, masked).

RedactionReason.description: Why a field was redacted from a bundle.

Registration.description:
An IS-04 **registration request**: the `{type, data}` body posted to a
Registry's `POST /x-nmos/registration/v1.3/resource`.

The `data` is the resource JSON for the declared `type`. Modelled internally
tagged on `type` (never `untagged`) so it round-trips robustly. The payload
is the raw resource JSON value so this one type carries any resource kind.

type.description: The kind of resource being registered.

RelaySetRequest.description: The `PUT /api/v1/mesh/relay` request body: the relay opt-in state.

enabled.description: Whether this machine should relay neighbours' heartbeats (brief §9.2).

body.description: The reply text to append to the thread.

Resource.description:
A persisted management resource: a stable `id`, a display `name`, and the
opaque `body` document (the config-as-code shape as canonical JSON).

body.description: The opaque resource document, as canonical JSON.

id.description: Stable resource id.

ResourceCore.description:
The IS-04 fields common to every resource.

Flattened into each resource type with `#[serde(flatten)]` so the wire JSON
is a flat object (as IS-04 defines), not a nested `core` object.

id.description: The resource UUID (a stable identifier, opaque string here).

tags.description: Free-form tags (each key maps to a list of string values, per IS-04).

version.description:
The TAI version `<seconds>:<nanoseconds>` ordering updates to this
resource (a newer version supersedes an older one).

ResourceInput.description:
The fields a create/update accepts (the id is supplied separately on create
and immutable on update).

name.description:
Human-friendly name. Optional on the wire: a create/update that omits it
(e.g. a programmatic seed that only carries the typed `body`) defaults to
an empty name rather than failing deserialization — the display name is a
label, never load-bearing, and callers that care always supply one.

ResourceType.description: The IS-04 resource type discriminator used by the registration protocol.

RevisionId.description: A monotonic, per-target revision number (starts at 1).

Role.description:
A coarse role governing which actions a principal may perform.

Ordered by privilege: `ReadOnly < Viewer < Operator < Admin`. [`Role::can`]
expresses the action gate.

`ReadOnly` and `Viewer` are both read-only at the action gate; they differ in
intent and ordering: `ReadOnly` is the floor (e.g. an audit/compliance role
that should never appear in a write-capable context), while `Viewer` is the
conventional observer. Keeping them distinct lets deployments and the
claims→role mapping express least privilege precisely without changing the
write gate.

to.description: The revision id to restore (a new revision is appended).

RouteClass.description:
The #11 class of a crosspoint take — the three tiers the capability matrix
defines, surfaced **before** the take applies.

Serialised in `snake_case` (`"class1"` / `"reset_lite"` / `"class2"`) and
**never `untagged`**. `#[non_exhaustive]` for forward compatibility.

RoutePlan.description:
The classified plan for a crosspoint take: the #11 class plus a
coerced-degradation flag.

coerced.description:
Whether the (would-be Class-2) take was **coerced** to Class-1 by an
operator-confirmed down/up-mix to the destination's pinned layout — a
Class-1 **with degradation**, surfaced so the operator sees the trade.

RouteTakeRequest.description:
The body of a `POST /routing/plan` or `/routing/{kind}/take` request.

Carries the destination + source, plus optional classifier hints used when the
engine snapshot does not (yet) carry the source inventory or the destination's
pinned params (so a plan/take is classifiable before the first probe):

* `source_channels` — the source audio track's channel count (used for the
  discrete-track layout comparison when the inventory is absent);
* `coerce` — the operator confirms a down/up-mix to the destination's pinned
  layout (turns a would-be Class-2 audio mismatch into Class-1-with-degradation);
* `primed` — whether the video target source is warm (absent ⇒ assumed primed).

coerce.description: Optional operator-confirmed coercion to the pinned layout.

primed.description: Optional video-target priming hint (absent ⇒ assumed primed).

source.description: The source elementary stream feeding the destination.

source_channels.description: Optional source audio channel-count hint (discrete-track layout compare).

oneOf.description: The mixed program-bus channel (absorbs the source layout).
A named discrete output track (its layout is pinned for the session).

pinned_channels.description: The pinned channel count, if known (the classifier's compare target).

RouteTargetDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_control::routing::RouteTarget`] (RT-11).

The destination a crosspoint take re-points — internally tagged on `kind`,
`snake_case`, never `untagged`.

`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_config::Salvo`].

Serde-equivalent field-for-field; the optional fields skip when empty/absent

layout.description: Recall a named layout (preset or head layout name).

ScanAccepted.description:
The `202 Accepted` body for a scan: the operation id correlating the scan and
the human-readable scope of what is browsed.

budget_ms.description: The scan time budget in milliseconds (the browse is always time-bounded).

note.description:
A reminder that discovery is **untrusted**: rows require explicit
confirm-adopt and discovery never creates a device (ADR-0041).

operation_id.description:
The operation id of the scan that actually runs — freshly started,
attached-to (single-flight), or replayed (`Idempotency-Key`). The
`device.discovered` rows it produces stream on the realtime `devices`
topic while it runs, each echoing this id as the envelope `corr`.

service_types.description:
The service types being browsed (Cast + NDI, plus any configured
zowietek-control type).

device_id.description: The id of the device this sender belongs to.

flow_id.description: The id of the flow this sender originates (opaque here).

manifest_href.description: The URL the sender's transport file (SDP) is served at.

Sender.description: An IS-04 **Sender**: an egress flow (a Multiview program/preview output).

pre-apply impact class, and the human-readable impact statement (ADR-M009 —
the API surfaces the impact BEFORE applying).

detail.description: The human-readable impact statement declared before apply.

impact.description:
The declared impact class: a device-side (`dev`) impact — the device
restarts its pipeline; Multiview program output is never interrupted.

operation_id.description:
The operation id correlating this mode change's eventual outcome on the
realtime stream (`device.mode`).

mode.description: The desired converged work mode (driver vocabulary, e.g. `encoder`).

allOf.description: The kind-specific payload (`kind` + its fields sit at top level).

`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::Source` — the body accepted by

`true` when the URL is unverified (operator-suppliable, undocumented by

`YouTube` live, resolved to HLS at runtime.
NDI input, bound by source name.

A ticket: a `CS-xxxx` id, the subject + severity, the auto-attached machine
context, an append-only update thread (opening body first), and the route +
state. The portal sync mirrors this local resource over the later transport.

route.description: The tier-derived support route this ticket was raised against.

ticket_id.description: The `CS-xxxx` ticket id (the portal correlation key).

updates.description: The append-only thread: the opening body, then every reply.

TicketContext.description:
The auto-attached machine context every ticket carries (§7.1): identity,
version, entitlement, ladder state, and the fingerprint score. **Reported,
never raw** — it carries the opaque tier + the salted fingerprint **score**
(a number), never raw serials/MACs (brief §8).

app_version.description: The application version (the build identity), always reported.

enforcement.description: The enforcement-ladder summary (the computed level), reported as data.

entitlement.description: The entitlement summary (the opaque tier + whether a lease is licensed).

fingerprint_score.description:
The salted hardware-fingerprint **score** (0–100, brief §2.3/§8) of the
active lease, or `None` when no lease is installed. A number, never raw.

TicketEnforcement.description:
The enforcement summary auto-attached to a ticket — the computed ladder level
(data, never control flow; brief §6).

TicketEntitlement.description:
The entitlement summary auto-attached to a ticket — the opaque tier + the
licensed flag (rendered, never computed; brief §1, O7).

licensed.description: Whether a verified lease is installed.

tier.description: The opaque commercial tier (rendered), or `none` when unlicensed.

TicketSeverity.description:
The operator-declared severity of a ticket (spec §11): `question`,
`degraded`, `blocking`. Serialised `lowercase` so the slug is stable across
the machine and the portal.

This is a **closed** set the spec pins to exactly these three levels (§11) —
deliberately **not** `#[non_exhaustive]` so a consumer (and the contract test)
can match it exhaustively. A new severity would be a contract change, not an
additive one.

TicketState.description:
The lifecycle state of a ticket. Local lifecycle is fully functional: an
operator can `close` a ticket they opened; a closed ticket refuses replies.

TicketSummary.description:
A ticket summary for the list surface (the thread is omitted to keep the
listing light; fetch a single ticket for the full thread).

updates.description: The number of updates on the thread.

TicketUpdate.description:
One immutable update on a ticket's append-only thread (the opening body or a
reply): who wrote it, when, and the text.

at_nanos.description: The media-timeline timestamp (nanoseconds) the update was recorded.

author.description:
The authenticated principal that wrote this update (its key id), or a
well-known support actor when the portal-fed sync lands later.

oneOf.description:
A wall-clock time-of-day in a zone; the next (down) or most-recent (up)
occurrence. `recur_daily` re-arms each day.

recur_daily.description: Re-arm to the next day's occurrence each day.

tz_offset_minutes.description: Fixed UTC offset in minutes (legacy / no-DST). Ignored when set.

oneOf.description: An absolute date+time `"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"` resolved in the zone.

at.description: Local wall-clock date+time (RFC3339 without a trailing zone).

tz_offset_minutes.description: Fixed UTC offset in minutes (legacy / no-DST). Ignored when set.

TimerTargetDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of `multiview_config::timer::TimerTarget` (tagged by
`target`; flattened into the `Timer` source variant alongside `kind`).

TransportParams.description:
One leg's RTP transport parameters (IS-05 supports up to two legs for
ST 2022-7 redundancy; this models one).

rtp_enabled.description: Whether RTP reception is enabled on this leg.

source_ip.description: The source IP for an `IGMPv3` source-specific-multicast filter.

TxtRecord.description: One advertised TXT key/value record (serializable, sortable).

value.description: The TXT value (decoded as UTF-8 by the browser).

cell.description: The cell id whose UMD label changes.

WarningCodeDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_events::WarningCode`] (SA-0 catalog).

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered as its `kebab-case` variant name.

WarningSeverityDoc.description:
`OpenAPI` mirror of [`multiview_events::WarningSeverity`] (SA-0).

Serde-equivalent: a unit enum rendered as its `snake_case` variant name.
